title: Easter Fun Cruise with Egg Hunt
tagline: Join us for an egg-citing Easter adventure!
description: Take the kids on an Easter cruise from Pier 40 along New York Harbor with views of the Brooklyn Bridge and lower Manhattan. Onboard activities for ages 3–12 include an egg hunt, balloon animals, face painting, Easter basket decorating, and a visit from the Easter Bunny. Tickets start around $75 for adults and $65 for children; kids get hands-on crafts and the thrill of finding eggs against the skyline.

title: Eid al-Fitr at Children's Museum of Manhattan
tagline: Celebrate the end of Ramadan with art, storytelling, and community.
description: Celebrate the end of Ramadan at the Children's Museum of Manhattan with a three-day Eid al-Fitr festival of art, stories, and performances. Kids ages 2–10 can make Moon Binoculars (for under 4), watercolor Well Wishes and moon art (5+), plus decorative lanterns and Eid Charity Boxes. Families will hear storytelling, watch dance, and take home crafts; activities are included with museum admission.

title: The Muppet Show
tagline: A brand-new special event with Kermit and the Muppets!
description: The Muppet Show returns in a 50th‑anniversary TV special on Disney+. Kermit, Miss Piggy and the gang mix new songs, comedy sketches and backstage chaos—plus guest Sabrina Carpenter—so kids (recommended 6–12) can laugh, sing along and enjoy classic puppetry. It’s a playful, music‑filled hour that shows how sketches and songs come together; requires a Disney+ subscription.
description_source: It's "The Muppet Show!" Kermit, Miss Piggy, and the beloved Muppet ensemble are back with a brand-new special event to celebrate the 50th anniversary of their iconic and groundbreaking original series which aired from 1975 to 1981. Keeping in the tradition of their classic variety show, the iconic Muppet characters return to television for the first time in over four decades with brand-new music, comedy sketches, and a whole lot of backstage chaos as they reintroduce themselves to long-standing...

title: Jim Henson Memorial Bench
tagline: A tribute to the joyful life of Jim Henson
description: A short stroll along Literary Walk in Central Park brings you to the Jim Henson Memorial Bench, a simple bench with a plaque honoring Henson's "joyful life" installed during the Mall's 1991 restoration. It's free to visit and easy for families to include on a park walk. Kids can sit, read the inscription and imagine Muppets while they play.
description_source: The Jim Henson Memorial Bench located in Central Park, NYC, celebrates the life of Jim Henson. It features a plaque that reads: 'TO THE JOYFUL LIFE OF JIM HENSON WHO LOVED THIS WALK IN THE PARK'. The bench is part of a series installed during the Mall's restoration in 1991, lining Literary Walk, a section of the park that maintains its natural aesthetic and is adorned with sculptures of notable figures.

title: Gillie and Marc Debut The Power of Togetherness Public Sculptures
tagline: A free public art exhibition featuring Sesame Street characters and endangered wildlife
description: Gillie and Marc’s free public sculpture show at 590 Madison Avenue places oversized Sesame Street characters (Elmo, Cookie Monster, Big Bird, Bert, Ernie) alongside large-scale endangered animals and the artists’ Rabbitwoman and Dogman. Kids ages 4–12 can walk around, spot playful details, and start conversations about animals and conservation. A family-friendly outdoor stop in Midtown.
description_source: Internationally acclaimed artists Gillie and Marc, in collaboration with Sesame Workshop, present The Power of Togetherness, a free public art exhibition at RXR’s 590 Madison Avenue. The immersive installation unites the artists’ iconic characters, Rabbitwoman and Dogman, with beloved Sesame Street figures, including Elmo, Cookie Monster, Big Bird, Bert, and Ernie, alongside endangered wildlife such as the Northern White Rhino, African Elephant, and Mountain Gorilla. title: RDMR BRKLYN's 2nd Annual Easter Egg Hunt and Picnic in Prospect Park
tagline: A relaxed afternoon of food, games, and community
description: Join RDMR BRKLYN's 2nd Annual Easter Egg Hunt and Picnic at Prospect Park Picnic House for an afternoon of food, games, and crafts. Egg hunts are split into 0–4 and 5–13 groups so kids hunt with peers; burgers and hot dogs are provided—please bring a picnic blanket and a side or dessert to share. Kids can try simple crafts and play group games; registration is required and admission is free.

title: Family Program: Build Upper Manhattan LEGO Event
tagline: Build Upper Manhattan using LEGO blocks!
description: Bring kids (ages 8–12) and build a scale model of Upper Manhattan with 100,000 LEGO bricks at P.S. 171. Families work together to recreate landmarks like Abyssinian Baptist Church and El Barrio’s Artspace PS109, learning about local architecture and community stories as they build. Doors 11:30 AM; program noon–2 PM; teams $40–$60.
description_source: Let’s build Upper Manhattan using 100,000 LEGO Blocks! Note: Doors open at 11:30 AM.

Free and rain or shine.

The event is benefiting the Foundation for Physical Therapy Research, encouraging support for important research in the field.

title: The Paper Bag Players: Hey There, Howdy, Everybody!
tagline: A joyful hour of family-friendly theater!
description: The Paper Bag Players perform a 60-minute kids' show at Kaye Playhouse with zany stories, paper-and-cardboard puppetry, songs, and audience participation. Children ages 3–9 can meet cats, galloping horses and cake-eating sleepwalkers while dancing and singing along. Free admission makes it an easy family outing in New York.
description_source: America’s beloved children’s theater company, THE PAPER BAG PLAYERS, presents a joyful new production: HEY THERE, HOWDY, EVERYBODY! Packed with zany stories, foot-stomping dances, audience participation, and paper-and-cardboard magic, kids will laugh, sing, and dance as they meet a parade of cats, galloping horses, cake-eating sleepwalkers, and more. It’s a funny, friendly, and thrilling hour of theater—perfect for children ages 3 through 9, and fun for the whole family!

Kids will be drawn into aerial moments and playful audience bits, and may get to try small tricks or clap along. Free and suitable for all ages, it’s a lively evening for the whole family.
description_source: Due to overwhelming success, Sunset Circus is BACK! Experience an immersive circus for audiences of all ages, presented by the physical theater company Parallel Exit. The extremely popular, New York Times-celebrated Sunset Circus features a company of 5-7 circus artists, including aerialists, jugglers, acrobats, and comedians. The sensational cast will entertain everyone, from kids to parents to grandparents, so bring the whole family!

tagline: A magical tale of love and sacrifice
description: A stage musical version of Hans Christian Andersen’s tale about a mermaid who trades her voice for legs to follow a prince. Expect sing-alongs, colorful under‑the‑sea scenes and lively dancing that draw kids into the story. Good for ages 3–10; children see themes of choices and identity up close. Tickets from $34 at The Players Theatre in NYC.

title: Visit a Brand-New Survivor Exhibit at The Paley Museum
tagline: Celebrate 50 seasons of Survivor
description: Walk through a new Paley Museum exhibit showing props, costumes and artifacts from all 50 seasons of Survivor, on view through May 31 (Wed–Sun, 12–6). Kids can spot challenge gear and costumes up close and imagine how the game works while parents enjoy the TV history. Tickets run about $16–$21.50.
description_source: In celebration of _Survivor’s_ landmark 50th season, The Paley Museum is presenting a dynamic, immersive exhibit honoring the iconic reality series now through May 31st. Featuring memorable props, iconic wardrobe pieces, and original artifacts spanning all fifty seasons, the exhibit offers fans a unique opportunity to relive Survivor’s most unforgettable moments and engage with the show in a whole new way. The Paley Museum is open every Wednesday – Sunday between 12 pm – 6 pm.

description: Bring kids ages 5–12 to The Twisted Spine in Brooklyn for playful, spooky poems and short readings from Bloodletter Magazine’s new issue. The pieces highlight trans voices in feminist horror with kid-friendly chills—think imaginative stories for fans of Goosebumps. Afterward you can meet some contributors at a casual reception; entry is free and proceeds support Advocates for Trans Equality.
description_source: Join us to celebrate the latest release of BLOODLETTER: A FEMINIST HORROR MAGAZINE! This one’s for the kids who skipped tag and read Goosebumps at recess. The evening will feature readings from Issue Six contributors followed by a reception. All proceeds were donated to Advocates for Trans Equality, a nonprofit organization fighting for the rights of trans people in America. Readers include: Megan M. Garwood, Gyasi Hall, Chelsea Lebron, Vanessa Meyer, Terry Nguyen, Veronica Rowan, Kailey, Ripley A. Mandanis, Dana Belott, Claire Orrange, Goldie Peacock, and An...

title: Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)
tagline: An interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s musical tale
description: Bring kids to Greenwich House Music School for a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf, where each character is represented by a different instrument. It's a short, kid-friendly show for ages 0–8 where children listen, laugh, and help guess who's who. After the performance, kids can try instruments at a petting zoo. Tickets free–$37.
description_source: Join us for Peter and the Wolf (and Friends!)—a playful, interactive retelling of Prokofiev’s beloved musical tale where each character comes to life through a different instrument. In this shorter, kid-friendly version, children will laugh, listen, and help guess who’s who as they meet Peter, his animal pals, and the not-so-scary wolf. But the fun doesn’t end there! Peter brings along a few surprise musical guests—other classical favorites designed to delight and engage the audience through interactive moments and an instrument petting zoo at the end.

title: Kiran Hargrave + Eli Rallo: Almost Life
tagline: Join us for a release event with award-winning, bestselling novelist Kiran Hargrave.
description: Come to a free book release and discussion at the Strand Book Store for Kiran Hargrave’s Almost Life, moderated by Ada Calhoun. The novel follows two women across decades, exploring young love, marriage and missed chances—good for teens (12+) and adults who like character-driven stories. Signed copies will be available and you'll hear the author talk about her process and themes.
description_source: Two young women meet in Paris one sultry summer in a decades-spanning tour de force about the enduring power of young love and the poignant heartbreak of missed chances... Erica and Laure’s love story spans decades, marriage, children, secret trysts, and the agonizing changes—both personal and political—that might mean they can be together.

title: Dead Father, Stepfather, Girl Father — A Comedy Show by A Chinese Queer Kid
tagline: A unique blend of comedy, drag, and political performance exploring queer identity and familial loss.
description: This solo show mixes stand-up, drag, burlesque and political theater as a Chinese queer performer digs into three decades of searching for father figures. Drawing on life in China, Taiwan and the U.S., it looks at grief, patriarchy and whether chosen family can replace biological ties. For ages 21+, tickets about $24–25 at Caveat.
description_source: It has been three decades since this only child from China began longing for approval and affection from multiple father figures—one deceased, one failed, and one who turns out to be more than just a father. After violence, grief, disappointment, and rebuilding, she/they begins to wonder: as a queer feminist, does one really need a father figure at all? After all, she has spent her entire life fighting patriarchy—personally and politically. Moving between China, the U.S., and in between, Taiwan,...

title: Ice Queen Performance
tagline: A frosty adventure in Central Park
description: See a 45-minute musical at Buttenwieser Hall about a brave girl crossing a wintry Central Park to rescue her friend from the Ice Queen. Original songs, clear storytelling and snowy visuals keep kids ages 2–12 engaged. Children follow the action, learn about teamwork and friendship, and sing along. Tickets start at $32.

This musical is 45 minutes long and best enjoyed by audience members ages 2+.

title: Anish Kapoor: Mirror Works
tagline: Explore the reflective world of Anish Kapoor
description: At Lisson Gallery, Anish Kapoor’s mirror installations use polished stainless steel to bend light and reflections across large sculptures. Children (recommended 6–12) can watch themselves warp and shift in the surfaces, notice how space stretches, and compare different views up close. Free entry makes it an easy visit to spark questions about perception and light.
description_source: Anish Kapoor’s work transcends traditional boundaries, encouraging viewers to engage with the environment around them through reflective materials. The exhibition features large-scale installations and intimate sculptures that invite observers to reconsider their own presence and experience in the art world, using light, reflection, and form to challenge spatial perception.

title: Faith Ringgold: Artist, Storyteller, Activist
tagline: Exploring diversity through literacy and storytelling.
description: This exhibition at the Sugar Hill Children's Museum displays Faith Ringgold’s paintings, illustrated scenes from MLK Jr.’s Letter from Birmingham Jail, working drawings and children’s-book manuscripts. Kids ages 5–12 can explore storytelling and social justice through bold images and story-quilt ideas, seeing how an artist turns thoughts into books. Free admission makes it easy to bring the family.
description_source: Artist, writer, educator and activist Faith Ringgold was born in Harlem, not far from the Sugar Hill Museum. It was here that she had her first art studio, became an art teacher and began to raise her own family. Her love for the children of Harlem inspired Broadway Housing Communities to create the museum. title: YOAH
tagline: Cirquework & Cluster Arts Global from Fukuoka, Japan
description: YOAH is a wordless contemporary circus from Japan mixing acrobatics, dance, traditional drumming and electronica with striking projections. Kids ages 7–12 will watch juggling, glowing diabolos and daring chair towers as a young wanderer meets Shinto spirit figures. At the New Victory Theater, children will feel the rhythm, see new circus skills up close, and leave inspired; tickets start at $25.
description_source: Established in 2021, Cirquework is a Japanese circus company founded by young circus artists active in Japan and other nations. Combining acrobatics, dance, music, lighting and visual effects, they create unique and innovative contemporary circus experiences for audiences. In the moonlit glow of dawn, a young wanderer named YOAH descends from her aerial silks into a circle of Shinto spirits, made manifest as mysterious maestros of cirque! Hypnotic projections play in sync with the performers as they send juggling balls soaring, conjure confidence atop a tower of chairs and fling their glowing diabolos to the heavens in hopeful arcs of neon. Set to a dramatic medley of traditional Japanese drumming and thumping electronica, this wordless symphony of circus will dazzle.

title: Women’s History Month: Story-Craft
tagline: Celebrate Women's History Month with books and crafts inspired by women artists
description: At Webster Library each week in March, kids ages 4–12 read picture books by women artists and then make crafts inspired by the stories. Children will try coloring, collaging, painting, drawing, sculpting, bookmarks and button-making with materials provided. Free; children 7 and under must be accompanied by a caregiver.
description_source: This event will take place in person at the Webster Library. Join us on a journey of exploration and creation. Each week we'll read a book from the collection and then jump into some crafting, based on or inspired by what we've read. Coloring, collaging, painting, drawing, sculpting, bookmark and button making — the only limit is our imagination! In March, we'll celebrate Women's History Month with books and crafts inspired by women artists. Open to ages 4-12. All children age 7 or under must be accompanied by a caregiver.
